name: Napalm_Mech

infobox.Systems: Firestorm Generator(Science)EffectFlaming artillery that drops fire on the way to its target. (Starts with 2-tile range)Upgrade I+1 Range ()Maximum range is increased by 1.Upgrade II+2 Range ()Maximum range is increased by 2.Heat Engines(Passive)EffectStanding on Fire removes Fire and gives the Mech "Boost".

sections.Team: This mech is available as part of theHeat Sinkerssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Napalm Mech is, in some ways, very similar to the Prime-classFlame Mech. It comes equipped with both a weapon that lights tiles on fire and pushes the last enemy away, as well as a passive that grants an immunity tofire tilesfor all Mechs.
The Heat Engines passive will let all Mechs consume Fire for a boost effect; when consumed, the fire will disappear from that tile. On the one hand, this means that Fire needs to be reapplied if looking to use it for limiting enemy movement; on the other hand, the Boost effect is very handy to have for the increase in weapon damage. Interestingly, the Boost effect can be gained from lava tiles in theVolcanic Hiveas well; though the lava won't be removed in this case, grounded Mechs will still be immune to fire and gain a boost from submerging themselves in lava.
The Napalm Mech can use its Firestorm Generator weapon to set up these boosts; the last tile set on fire will also be pushed away, which can be useful for repositioning an ally or enemy. Compared toFlame Thrower, note that Firestorm Generator will not damage enemies that are already on fire, but it does start with a higher threat range of 2 tiles and has higher potential range; it can similarly be used to shoot through buildings and push enemies on the other side, as fire does not damage buildings. Firestorm Generator can also be used to set spawn points on fire, which will force emerging Vek to suffer burn damage on the turn they emerge before attacking.
Upgrading the range of Firestorm Generator will allow greater flexibility for the other Mechs in the Squad to set up boosts; the first upgrade for+1 Rangeonly costs 1 Reactor Core. Once the Napalm Mech gets a 2nd Reactor Core, the first upgrade can be disabled, and its Reactor ore can reallocated with the new one to power+2 Range.
If looking to use the Napalm Mech in aCustom Squad, consider pairing it with Mechs that get the most out of the damage increase; a notable example being theSiege Mech, which has a high damaging and displacement range with its default Cluster Artillery weapon.
